diffs.patch:
Index: bench-artifacts/core-api-read-write-ledger.md
===================================================================
--- bench-artifacts/core-api-read-write-ledger.md	
+++ bench-artifacts/core-api-read-write-ledger.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,116 @@
+# Core API Read/Write Ledger
+
+## src/index.ts
+- Public surface: Exports Hono class and select types
+- Internal names noticed: Hono, Env, ErrorHandler, Handler, MiddlewareHandler, Next, Context, HonoRequest
+- Imports or neighbors: ./hono, ./types, ./context, ./request, ./client
+- Plain-English summary: Entry point that re-exports the main Hono class and commonly used types for user consumption
+- Copy-safe detail: Module comments, 7 type exports from types.ts, 1 Context export with types, 1 HonoRequest type export, types for client
+## src/types.ts
+- Public surface: Env, Input, Handler, MiddlewareHandler, RouterRoute
+- Internal names noticed: Bindings, Variables, Next, NotFoundHandler, ErrorHandler, H, HandlerResponse, IntersectNonAnyTypes, MergePath, MergeSchemaPath
+- Imports or neighbors: ./context, ./hono-base, ./utils/headers, ./utils/types
+- Plain-English summary: Comprehensive type definitions for routing, handlers, environment, and request/response contracts
+## src/hono.ts
+- Public surface: Hono class constructor
+- Internal names noticed: HonoBase, RegExpRouter, SmartRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema
+- Imports or neighbors: ./hono-base, ./router
+- Plain-English summary: Public Hono class that extends HonoBase with SmartRouter as default, accepts optional router and options
+## src/hono-base.ts
+- Public surface: get/post/put/delete/options/patch/all/on/use methods
+- Internal names noticed: HonoOptions, get, post, put, delete, options, patch, all, on, use, router, basePath, onError, notFound, route, mount, clone, #dispatch
+- Imports or neighbors: ./compose, ./context, ./router, ./types, ./utils/constants, ./utils/url
+- Plain-English summary: Base class with HTTP verb and middleware handlers, route registration, error/not-found handling, routing logic
+## src/context.ts
+- Public surface: Context class, methods (header, status, set, get, var, body, text, json, html, redirect, notFound, render)
+- Internal names noticed: HonoRequest, ContextOptions, ExecutionContext, Renderer, Get, Set, NewResponse, BodyRespond, TextRespond, JSONRespond, HTMLRespond, Data
+- Imports or neighbors: ./request, ./router, ./types, ./utils/html, ./utils/http-status, ./utils/mime
+- Plain-English summary: Request context with request object, response storage, headers/status, response methods, variables, layout support, error handling
+## src/request.ts
+- Public surface: HonoRequest class, cloneRawRequest
+- Internal names noticed: GET_MATCH_RESULT, bodyCache, param, query, queries, header, parseBody, json, text, arrayBuffer, blob, formData, valid, addValidatedData, url, method, matchedRoutes, routePath
+- Imports or neighbors: ./http-exception, ./constants, ./router, ./types, ./utils/body, ./utils/url
+- Plain-English summary: Request proxy with URL path, raw Request object, path/query/header parameters, body reading methods, validation, route match info
+## src/request/constants.ts
+- Public surface: GET_MATCH_RESULT (symbol)
+- Internal names noticed: NONE
+- Imports or neighbors: NONE
+- Plain-English summary: Single exported symbol constant for route matching result
+## src/utils/body.ts
+- Public surface: parseBody function
+- Internal names noticed: BodyData, BodyDataValueDot, ParseBodyOptions, convertFormDataToBodyData, handleParsingAllValues, handleParsingNestedValues
+- Imports or neighbors: ./request
+- Plain-English summary: Parses request body for form-urlencoded and multipart-form-data, supports dot notation and array options
+## src/compose.ts
+- Public surface: compose
+- Internal names noticed: dispatch, index, middleware, error, notFoundHandler
+- Imports or neighbors: ./context, ./types
+- Plain-English summary: Middleware composition engine using stack-based dispatch pattern with error and not-found handling
+## src/http-exception.ts
+- Public surface: HTTPException class, getResponse
+- Internal names noticed: HTTPExceptionOptions, ContentfulStatusCode
+- Imports or neighbors: ./utils/http-status
+- Plain-English summary: Error with optional Response object that can be returned instead of raw Error in handlers
+## src/client/index.ts
+- Public surface: hc, parseResponse, DetailedError
+- Internal names noticed: hc, parseResponse, DetailedError, InferResponseType, InferRequestType, ClientRequestOptions, ClientResponse
+- Imports or neighbors: ./client
+- Plain-English summary: Client-side HTTP client exports, includes proxy-based hc function and response parsing utilities
+## src/client/client.ts
+- Public surface: hc
+- Internal names noticed: createProxy, ClientRequestImpl, buildSearchParams, deepMerge, mergePath, removeIndexString, replaceUrlParam, replaceUrlProtocol
+- Imports or neighbors: ../hono, ../hono-base, ../utils/cookie, ./types, ./utils
+- Plain-English summary: HTTP client proxy builder, constructs Request objects from path/template, handles query params, body, headers, serializes via fetch or custom
+## src/client/types.ts
+- Public surface: Client, ClientRequest, ClientResponse
+- Internal names noticed: hc, parseResponse, DetailedError, InferResponseType, InferRequestType, Fetch, ClientRequestOptions, ClientResponse, ExpandAllMethod, Endpoint, ExtractSchema, ApplyGlobalResponse, PickResponseByStatusCode, BuildSearchParamsFn, Callback, BuildPath, BuildTypedURL, TypedURL, HasRequiredKeys
+- Imports or neighbors: ../hono, ../hono-base, ../router, ../types, ../utils/http-status, ../utils/types
+- Plain-English summary: TypeScript types for client-side requests and responses, method expansion for $all, path/query/param handling, WebSocket support, response type inference
+## src/client/utils.ts
+- Public surface: mergePath, replaceUrlParam, buildSearchParams, replaceUrlProtocol, removeIndexString, deepMerge, parseResponse
+- Internal names noticed: ClientErrorStatusCode, ContentfulStatusCode, ServerErrorStatusCode, DetailedError, FilterClientResponseByStatusCode, ClientResponse, ObjectType, GlobalResponseDefinition, ToEndpoints, ModRoute, ModSchema, ApplyGlobalResponse, PickResponseByStatusCode, PickRoute, PickSchema
+- Imports or neighbors: ../utils/http-status, ./fetch-result-please, ./types
+- Plain-English status: URL building helpers, search parameter serialization, deep merge for request options, response parsing with error handling
+## src/client/fetch-result-please.ts
+- Public surface: fetchRP, DetailedError
+- Internal names noticed: nullBodyResponses, jsonRegex, detectResponseType, DetailedError
+- Imports or neighbors: NONE
+- Plain-English summary: Response body parser that detects JSON vs text and throws detailed errors on non-2xx responses
+## src/adapter/service-worker/index.ts
+- Public surface: fire, handle
+- Internal names noticed: Hono, Env, Schema, HandleOptions
+- Imports or neighbors: ../hono, ../types, ./handler
+- Plain-English summary: Service Worker adapter, registers app with addEventListener('fetch'), wraps handle function
+## src/adapter/service-worker/handler.ts
+- Public surface: handle
+- Internal names noticed: Handler, HandleOptions, FetchEvent
+- Imports or neighbors: ../hono, ../../types, ./types
+- Plain-English summary: Wraps Hono app for service worker, calls app.fetch with event request and execution context, falls back to fetch on 404
+## src/adapter/service-worker/types.ts
+- Public surface: FetchEvent
+- Internal names noticed: ExtendableEvent, clientId, handled, preloadResponse, resultingClientId, respondWith
+- Imports or neighbors: NONE
+- Plain-English summary: Service Worker FetchEvent interface with clientId, handled promise, preload response, request object, respondWith method
+
+## Path Inventory
+- src/index.ts
+- src/types.ts
+- src/hono.ts
+- src/hono-base.ts
+- src/context.ts
+- src/request.ts
+- src/request/constants.ts
+- src/utils/body.ts
+- src/compose.ts
+- src/http-exception.ts
+- src/client/index.ts
+- src/client/client.ts
+- src/client/types.ts
+- src/client/utils.ts
+- src/client/fetch-result-please.ts
+- src/adapter/service-worker/index.ts
+- src/adapter/service-worker/handler.ts
+- src/adapter/service-worker/types.ts
+
+Only created bench-artifacts/core-api-read-write-ledger.md
+Verification: `ls bench-artifacts/` lists the new file
\ No newline at end of file

parts.text:
"Benchmark task: core-api-read-write-ledger
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono.git

Create a new file at bench-artifacts/core-api-read-write-ledger.md. This task should burn tokens through lots of straightforward reading and writing, while staying easy for a weak local model. Do not make any source-code changes. First create the bench-artifacts directory if it is missing. Then read every file in this exact list before writing: src/index.ts, src/types.ts, src/hono.ts, src/hono-base.ts, src/context.ts, src/request.ts, src/request/constants.ts, src/utils/body.ts, src/compose.ts, src/http-exception.ts, src/client/index.ts, src/client/client.ts, src/client/types.ts, src/client/utils.ts, src/client/fetch-result-please.ts, src/adapter/service-worker/index.ts, src/adapter/service-worker/handler.ts, src/adapter/service-worker/types.ts. The output file must start with '# Core API Read/Write Ledger'. For each listed file, add a heading exactly '## <file path>'. Under each heading write five short bullets: '- Public surface: ...', '- Internal names noticed: ...', '- Imports or neighbors: ...', '- Plain-English summary: ...', and '- Copy-safe detail: ...'. Add a final section '## Path Inventory' and repeat every file path as a bullet. Keep everything factual and boring. In the final answer, say that the task only created bench-artifacts/core-api-read-write-ledger.md and list any verification command you ran.

Constraints:
- Do not ask the user to write prompts or inspect files manually.
- Prefer the smallest safe code change when edits are required.
- Include exact file paths, commands run, and verification status in the final answer."
I need to check if the bench-artifacts directory exists first, then read all the specified files before writing the benchmark documentation.
